profile.title: Your profile
profile.summary: Update your contact info, set up MFA, and manage emergency contacts.

contact_info.title: Contact information
contact_info.summary: Update your name, email, phone number, and address here. Click Save when you are done.
contact_info.details: Your email address is used for notifications and password resets. Keep it current so you do not miss important messages.

avatar.title: Profile photo
avatar.summary: Upload a profile photo. JPG or PNG, up to 2 MB.
avatar.details: Your photo is optional. It appears next to your name in messages and in the admin user list.

mfa.title: Multi-Factor Authentication
mfa.summary: Enable MFA for extra security. This adds a second login step using a code from your phone. Highly recommended for admin and medical accounts.
mfa.details: After enabling MFA, you will be asked to scan a QR code with an authenticator app. Each time you log in, you will enter a 6-digit code from the app. If you lose access to your authenticator, contact a super admin.

emergency_contacts.title: Emergency contacts
emergency_contacts.summary: Add at least one emergency contact. The first contact you add becomes your primary.
emergency_contacts.details: Emergency contacts are only used if camp staff need to reach someone on your behalf — for example, in a medical situation. They are not displayed publicly.

what_is_mfa.question: What is MFA?
what_is_mfa.answer: MFA stands for Multi-Factor Authentication. It adds a second step to your login: after your password, you enter a 6-digit code from an authenticator app on your phone. This way, even if someone learns your password, they cannot log in without your phone.

why_emergency_contact.question: Why do I need an emergency contact?
why_emergency_contact.answer: In case camp staff need to reach someone on your behalf — for example, if there is a medical situation and you are unreachable. At least one contact is strongly recommended.

mfa_disabled.message: MFA is disabled. Set it up to protect your account.

settings.title: App settings
settings.summary: Adjust language, notifications, and accessibility preferences.

language.title: Language
language.summary: Change between English and Spanish using the language toggle in the header or from this page.
language.details: The language setting applies to all pages in the portal. Some content submitted by staff may remain in the original language.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.summary: Choose which events send you a notification: application updates, announcements, messages, and deadlines.
notifications.details: You can turn individual notification types on or off. Turning off a notification type means you will not receive an alert for those events — check the portal manually to stay up to date.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.summary: Adjust font size, enable high contrast mode, or reduce motion if animations bother you.
accessibility.details: These settings apply only to your account and are saved across sessions. If you use a screen reader, the portal supports standard ARIA attributes throughout.

delete_account.title: Delete account
delete_account.summary: Permanently deletes your account and all associated data. This cannot be undone.
delete_account.details: If you delete your account, your applications, documents, and message history are also removed. Contact camp staff before deleting if you have submitted applications or documents that may still be needed.

change_password.question: Where can I change my password?
change_password.answer: Go to your Profile page and look for the Security section. You can update your password there at any time.

notification_not_arriving.question: Why is my notification not arriving?
notification_not_arriving.answer: First check that the notification type is enabled in this Settings page. If it is enabled, check your email spam folder. If you still have trouble, contact camp staff.

inbox.title: Your inbox
inbox.summary: Read messages, reply, and stay on top of conversations.

reading.title: Reading messages
reading.summary: Click any conversation to open it. Unread conversations are bolded in the list.
reading.details: Once you open a thread it is marked as read automatically. You can mark a message unread again from the thread actions menu.

reply.title: Replying
reply.summary: Type in the box at the bottom of the conversation and click Send. Use Reply to respond to the original sender, or Reply All to include everyone in the thread.
reply.details: Reply All excludes BCC recipients — they will not be notified of your reply, which is by design to preserve privacy.

compose.title: Composing a new message
compose.summary: Click "+ New Message" to start a new conversation. Search for recipients by name or email address.
compose.details: You can add multiple recipients. Use the To field for primary recipients. CC is for others who should be kept informed but do not need to act.

mark_important_starred.title: Organizing your inbox
mark_important_starred.summary: Star, mark important, or archive conversations to keep your inbox tidy. Archived threads can be unarchived at any time.
mark_important_starred.details: Starred threads appear in the Starred folder. Important affects sort order. Archiving moves the thread out of the inbox without deleting it.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.summary: Drag a file into the compose window, or click the paperclip icon. PDFs and images (JPG, PNG) are supported.
attachments.details: Attachments are uploaded when you send. There is no preview before sending. Make sure you are attaching the right file before clicking Send.

star_vs_important.question: What is the difference between Star and Important?
star_vs_important.answer: Star is a personal bookmark — it moves the thread to your Starred folder for quick access. Important affects the sort order so high-priority threads surface near the top of your inbox. You can apply both.

unarchive.question: Can I un-archive a thread?
unarchive.answer: Yes. Open the Archived folder, find the thread, and click Unarchive. The thread moves back to your inbox.
